channels.name: fan-favorite-voting
channels.topic:
Want to be considered for the Fan Favorite Award for the Whisk Plushie Competition? 

**RULES:** 
- You must first submit your plushie to the official contest at **https://labs.google/whisk-plushie-challenge**
 - Only 1 entry per person 
- Upload a file of your plushie. We do not accept links.
- The "Fan Favorite Entry" tag must be attached to your submission 
- Do not include sensitive or personal information. 
- Revisit the #rules-and-guidelines before uploading. 

Submissions that do not meet the above guidelines will not be eligible for the Fan Favorite Award. We can't wait to see what plushies everyone created! <:labs_heart:1252324111363870720>

channels.topic:
File your bugs here!

- Share what you're trying to do
- What did you expect to happen?
- What happened instead (the bug behavior)
- Share any details that you can, without sharing personal information (don't show screenshots with your email, name, phone number, etc)

channels.name: 📋-feature-requests
channels.topic:
Share your feature requests for NotebookLM! 

- Describe what you're trying to do and why
- If possible, how do you accomplish this task today?
- Are there any best practices for this particular feature that you recommend?

Apply tags to help others more easily scan your request:
- Sources: improvements to how sources work, requesting additional source types, etc
- Research: features that help you do research more effectively
- Writing: features that help you write more effectively
- Chat: improvements to the LLM or underlying model in NotebookLM

channels.name: student-hub
channels.topic:
Hey everyone! This channel is your dedicated space to connect with fellow students using NotebookLM from all over the world, and chat directly with the NotebookLM team!

**Stay Respectful:** This is a diverse, global community—let's be kind and supportive in all interactions.

**Feedback First: **Found a cool new way to use NotebookLM? Share it here! Experiencing issues? Let us know, and we’ll help out.

**Collaborate & Network:**  Introduce yourself! Let us know your name, what you're studying, and which university you're at. Use this space to exchange ideas, find collaborators, and connect with students from other universities.

**Be Specific:** When reporting an issue or suggesting a feature, provide as much detail as possible to help the team understand and address it quickly.

channels.name: audioldm2
channels.topic:
Tips and Tricks for better audio!

1. Try to use more adjectives to describe your sound. For example: "A man is speaking clearly and slowly in a large room" is better than "A man is speaking". This can make sure AudioLDM 2 understands what you want.

2. Try to use different random seeds, which can affect the generation quality significantly sometimes.

3. It's better to use general terms like 'man' or 'woman' instead of specific names for individuals or abstract objects that humans may not be familiar with, such as 'mummy'.

HuggingBot is a beta feature; The AudioLDM2 model can be found here: https://huggingface.co/spaces/haoheliu/audioldm2-text2audio-text2music

channels.name: pr-thunderdome-dangerzone
channels.topic:
We have about 30 PRs and issues every hour. We are using AI to close them. This allows the limited attention span of the maintainers to be preserved. 

This channel is an escape hatch: if you had a REALLY GOOD (and we mean really good) PR, that got auto-closed, here is your opportunity to talk to a human about it.

We value all the contributions to the project - which is why this channel exists. 

But with great power comes great responsibility. You need to be professional, and respect the maintainers time. You must explain why *your PR specifically* deserves the maintainers attention over 1000 others. If you do that? Great! We can ship some great stuff together.

But if you are disrespectful, lazy, or otherwise annoying, the 🚨 iron fist of moderation 🚨 will come down hard on you. This means mutes 🔕, warnings ⚠️, or bans 🔨 on your first offence. Most people are sensible - but a lot aren't - so we must be strict. Don't take it personally!

Slowmode is on - you get one post every 6 hours. Make it count!

scheduled_events.name: [Watch Party] Wednesday Build Hour: Inside Gemma: A Live Build with the Google DeepMind Product Team
scheduled_events.description:
Register Now - https://goo.gle/inside-gemma

Join us for this Wednesday Build Hour to explore the most advanced capabilities of Google's open models. Led by a Product Manager directly from the Gemma team, this session offers an exclusive look at what the state-of-the-art weights can truly do.


We’ll walk through a live technical demo where you'll see these models in action across real-world scenarios.

scheduled_events.name: [Watch Party] Wednesday Build Hour: Choose your own Cloud Run Adventure
scheduled_events.description:
Register Now- https://goo.gle/-cloud-run-adventure


Welcome to our Wednesday Build Hour, your new weekly appointment for technical growth and community connection across EMEA.
This week we’ll learn about how Cloud Run supports many different kinds of workloads and how you can get them up and running without ever building a container. By leveraging Google Cloud’s source-to-run capabilities, you can simply point to your code repository and let the platform handle the heavy lifting of packaging and deployment.

scheduled_events.name: [Watch Party] Wednesday Build Hour: Build and Deploy to Google Cloud with Antigravity
scheduled_events.description:
Register Now - https://goo.gle/google-cloud-antigravity

In this codelab, you will learn how to use Google Antigravity to design, build, and deploy a serverless application to Google Cloud. We will build a serverless and event-driven document pipeline that ingests files from Google Cloud Storage (GCS), processes them using Cloud Run and Gemini, and streams their metadata into BigQuery.

scheduled_events.name: [Watch Party] Wednesday Build Hour: Detecting and Editing Visual Objects with Gemini and Nano Banana
scheduled_events.description:
Register Now  - https://goo.gle/gemini-and-nano-banana


In this codelab, you will learn how to leverage the power of multimodal AI to automate complex image manipulation workflows. We will build an intelligent pipeline that uses Gemini to identify specific visual objects within an image, employs Nano Banana to surgically extract and restore those elements, and finishes by applying generative edits to transform the final output.

scheduled_events.name: [Build with AI] Create a Real-Time Multiplayer Speed Game
scheduled_events.description:
Join us for a hands-on workshop where we’ll build a multiplayer "Fact or Cap" game from scratch! In this beginner-friendly session, you will learn how to use Gemini AI to generate dynamic questions, and how to turn Google Sheets into a free, real-time database for a live leaderboard. 

At the end of the workshop, we’ll test our project live by dropping the game link directly into the Discord Stage chat so everyone can play. 

Come learn new skills and compete for the top score!

scheduled_events.name: [Watch Party] Cloud Next '26 Opening Keynote
scheduled_events.description:
Join us on Discord for a community watch party of the Next '26 Opening Keynote!

We’ll be following Google Cloud CEO Thomas Kurian as he unveils major product announcements and showcases how global industry leaders are driving real-world impact with Google Cloud.

Grab your seat in the Stage Channel and get ready!
To watch the Opening Keynote with ASL:  https://goo.gle/cloud-next26-openingkeynote-asl

scheduled_events.name: [Watch Party] Cloud Next '26 Developer Keynote
scheduled_events.description:
It’s time to move beyond the basics and unlock the true creative power of the modern developer. We’re moving past "Hello World" and diving straight into the deep end of engineering.

Join us for a Discord watch party as we spend the next hour slinging code to solve the hardest problems in the game: multi-agent orchestration, durable memory, and zero-trust security. We aren't just talking about the future; we’re using AI to build AI agents that run platforms autonomously and model the world at scale.

To watch the Developer Keynote with ASL: https://goo.gle/next-developer-keynote-asl

- Be fair! Only post **once every 7 days**!
- For your own safety, discuss job details and private information in **direct messages**.
- When hiring, include **expectations, job requirements, and compensation details**
- Be polite please. All of our usual rules apply.

If you don't follow the rules, your post may be deleted on sight and a strike against your profile will be noted. Repeated breaking of these rules may result in a temporary or permanent ban.

**Banned content:** Interview impersonation, scams, and applying under false pretenses are explicitly bannable offenses. Please be mindful of scammers and please don't agree to requests to account sharing, interview impersonations, or remote-desktop proxying.

**Disclaimer:** No postings on this board are vetted or endorsed by Expo.
If a job posting looks suspicious, please tag or DM an Expo team member.

Selection: If you're a match, we’ll reach out via email your provided in the next 1-2 business days.
Scheduling: You’ll pick a slot that fits your schedule. We will send you a meeting invite with 1:1 video chat link via Google Meet.

scheduled_events.name: Introducing notebooks in Gemini
scheduled_events.description:
With notebooks in Gemini, you can now keep multiple projects organized in one place, and even add past chats, relevant files, and Notebook LM notebooks as sources. Join us for our next community event as Product Manager at Google DeepMind, Rebecca Zapfel, demos how to organize, add multimodal context, and take advantage of persistent memory with notebooks.

Notebooks in Gemini are rolling out, starting with Google AI Ultra, Pro, and Plus subscribers on the web. In the coming weeks we'll expand access to mobile, more countries across Europe and to free users.

channels.name: supauser-escalate
channels.topic:
Use this area to report tickets that you believe need to be escalete by the Supabase team.

Requirements:
- OP has reported an issue that blocks them from using the platform and the Supabase team are the only ones that can help.
- OP has confirmed they reported a ticket and provided a ticket number.

Some examples of thing that need to be escalate:
- User can't access the dashboard and are reporting urgency due to apps in production, or issues with customers.
- Project has failed to restore properly after a backup restoration or after resuming its paused status.
- Project has failed to start after creation and can't connect to it.

***When posting, always include the link to the original post.***

channels.name: help-and-questions
channels.topic:
Welcome to the Supabase help channel!

Here you will find the requests for help from the community. Don't hesitate to contribute!

**tl;dr;**

1. **Ask your question**
   - Use a clear, descriptive title.
   - Describe what you’re trying to achieve and what’s going wrong.
   - Share relevant environment details: OS, framework, platform (web/mobile), and library version (e.g. `@supabase/supabase-js@X.Y.Z` or `@supabase/ssr@X.Y.Z`).
   - Tag your post with the appropriate category (see **“Tagging your question”** below).
2. **Help others**
   - Browse recent questions and lend a hand when you can.
3. **Share your solution**
   - Once your issue is resolved, add the **Solved** tag and post your answer so it benefits future readers.
4. **Server etiquette**
   - Please don't tag **supabase team** members on the platform. Please don't tag **mods** unless someone is spamming the server or breaking server rules.

---

### Channels

- #help-and-questions – All Supabase-related questions
- #off-topic – General chat and non-Supabase topics

### Before You Post

1. **Read the #rules.**
2. **Search existing posts** to avoid duplicates.
3. **Check the documentation**
   - Official docs: https://supabase.com/docs
   - Troubleshooting: https://github.com/orgs/supabase/discussions/categories/troubleshooting

### Tagging Your Question
Tag your question appropriately, for example:

- `CLI` for local development specific questions
- `self-hosted` for hosting your own Supabase instance questions
- `Python` for Supabase python library questions

> **Tip:** Always wrap code in fenced blocks (```), not screenshots—see https://www.markdownguide.org/extended-syntax/#fenced-code-blocks — for easier reading and copying.

### No Response Yet?

1. **Bump your thread** by adding a follow-up comment.
2. **Check your [XY problem](https://xyproblem.info/)**: Are you describing your real goal or a premature solution?
3. **Provide a minimal repro** on GitHub, focusing on the issue rather than your entire app.

### Marking as Solved

1. Apply the **Solved** tag.
2. Thank those who helped you.
3. For feedback or off-topic discussions, head to #off-topic.

### Server etiquette

1. Please don't tag **supabase team** members on the platform. 
2. Please don't tag **mods** unless someone is spamming the server or breaking server rules.
3. Please don't tag **mods** if you they aren't already a part of the discussion you are currently having or they have said you are free to do so.

channels.name: jobs
channels.topic:
This channel is dedicated to finding people for your Supabase projects. There are 3 tags:
 - Looking for help: if you are looking for someone to help you with a project, with optional compensation.
 - Looking for job: you are looking for a job, preferably related to Supabase.
 - Offering job: if you are looking for someone to recruit against payment.

All job offerings should be Supabase related otherwise they will be removed from this channel.

## Job Posting Requirements

All job postings **must** include the following information:

- The name of the company or hiring agency  
- Compensation details  
- A clear and complete description of the role  
- Direct involvement with Supabase  

Any post submitted to this channel that does not constitute a legitimate Supabase-related job listing will be removed. Multiple postings related to the same role or opportunity are not permitted.

Threaded replies that are not relevant to the original job posting will be removed. Users who repeatedly submit irrelevant or noncompliant content may be prohibited from posting in this channel.

If you suspect fraudulent activity, please contact a moderator and provide appropriate supporting evidence.

channels.name: omarchy-help
channels.topic:
**BEFORE POSTING: Please search for your issue using the search at the top of the screen. It is very likely your issue has already been encountered and there is a thread with the answer you need.**

After searching, if you do not find the answer, create a **new** thread and provide details to aid in assistance including:
- Which installation method are you using? [ISO vX.X.X | Manual Script]
- What are you installing onto? [ VirtualBox | VMWare | Parallels | MacBook | PC ]

If you have encountered an error during install, select the **View Full Log** option and include snapshots of that or upload the logs and provide a link if the machine has internet access at the time of install.

The more details and log information you can provide, the faster and higher likelihood of resolution.

Tag your post appropriately to make it easier to filter and find. Use 🛟 Help Needed for any new posts seeking help.

To help us keep things clean, once your post is saisfied, please remove the 🛟 Help Needed tag, and **Close** the post. That will leave the post available to others but move it to the bottom to make way for newer issues.

Remember; everyone here is a volunteer using their free time to help fellow community members.

channels.topic:
**Guidelines:**
1. Our [Open Source Community Guidelines](https://aws.github.io/code-of-conduct) and [Code of Conduct](https://aws.amazon.com/codesofconduct/) apply in this server. 
2. Please, do not ping or reach out to staff directly on DM's. Rather use this channel to get the widest possible participation. The members will use their best effort to answer questions and capture feedback.
3. Remember to never share AWS credentials or any sensitive information in this server.
4. Please do not spam the channel with any personal messages, job postings, or unrelated information.
5. Before creating a new post, please search for an existing post that matches your questions.

**Tags**
Please tag each post which should have one of the following:

Kiro Agent
- `chat` for agentic chat, context, and conversation questions
- `specs` for specification creation, management, and implementation questions
- `hooks` for automation, triggers, and workflow questions  
- `steering` for project guidance, rules, and configuration questions
- `mcp` for Model Context Protocol server setup and integration questions
- `powers` for powers related questions

Kiro Clients
- `ide` for code editor based questions
- `cli` for CLI based questions
- `autonomous-agent` for Kiro autonomous agent
- `setup` for installation, configuration, and getting started questions
- `extensions` for extensions and external tool connections
- `terminal` for terminal related questions
- `autocomplete` for autocomplete code feature

Models
- `models` for model related questions

Account
- `authentication` for login and auth questions
- `billing` for billing related questions

Platforms
- `mac` for MacOS platform
- `windows` for Windows platform
- `linux` for Linux platform

For all other questions
- `general` for other Kiro-related discussions and feedback

channels.name: 👀glm-image
channels.topic:
We are thrilled to open source GLM-Image! 🎉
We’ve built something special, a visual model powered by our innovative "Autoregressive + Diffusion Decoder" hybrid architecture.
Why is this cool? It brings together the power of Language Models and Image Generation. This combination allows GLM-Image to balance global instruction understanding with precise local detailing, effectively overcoming the challenges of generating knowledge-intensive visuals like Posters, PPT slides, and Scientific Infographics.
Code & Docs: [github.com/zai-org/GLM-Image](https://github.com/zai-org/GLM-Image)
Experience it right now: http://huggingface.co/zai-org/GLM-Image
In this channel: Have fun, share your demos, and let's experience it together! 🚀

channels.name: 😔-bad-case-report
channels.topic:
See something acting funky?
Welcome to the bug-hunting zone! 
🐞 If you’ve spotted a glitch, weird behavior, or something just feels “off,” drop it here so we can squash it fast and keep Z.ai smooth.

How to be an awesome bug hunter:
🔍 Tell the story – What went wrong? Give us the details so we can see what you see.
📸 Show the evidence – Screenshots, screen recordings, or even a quick GIF help us big time.
🤝 Keep it chill & helpful – We’re all about fixing things fast, and your feedback makes that possible.

Thanks for being part of the squad! ✨ Let’s keep Z.ai bug-free together! 🚀

channels.name: support-reader
channels.topic:
Your LLMs deserve better input.

Reader does two things:
1. Read: It converts any URL to an LLM-friendly input with https://r.jina.ai/https://your.url. Get improved output for your agent and RAG systems at no cost.

2. Search: It searches the web for a given query with https://s.jina.ai/your+query. This allows your LLMs to access the latest world knowledge from the web.

channels.topic:
**Before You Post**

Search the forum and check [closed GitHub issues](https://github.com/ComposioHQ/composio/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20state%3Aclosed) first. Your question might already be answered.

**What This Forum Is For**

Questions and discussions about developing with Composio—our SDKs, tooling, integrations, and platform features.

**Getting Good Help**

The more context you provide, the better answers you'll get. Include:

**General Info**
- What you're trying to build
- What's not working
- Steps to reproduce the issue
- A minimal demo repo if possible

**Error Details**
- Full error message and traceback
- Function/method where it failed
- Screenshots if relevant

**Environment**

Python SDK:
- Python version
- Composio library (e.g. `composio_langchain`) and version
- OS
- Your `requirements.txt`

JavaScript SDK:
- Node version
- `composio-core` version
- OS
- Your `package.json`

**Auth & Credentials**

Are you using your own OAuth credentials or Composio's defaults?

**Reference**

Check our [GitHub issue template](https://github.com/ComposioHQ/composio/issues) for examples. Partial information = slower help.

channels.name: help-forum
channels.topic:
# How do I ask a good question?

We’re happy to help you, but in order to improve your chances of getting an answer, here are some guidelines to follow:

## Make sure your question is related to Crawl4AI, LLMs or AI in general
We only accepts certain types of questions related to Crawl4AI and the purpose it serves,. If your question is not on-topic or is otherwise unsuitable for this server, then it will likely be closed.

Closure is not the end of the road for questions; it is intended to be a temporary state until the question is revised to meet our requirements. However, if you fail to do that, or it is impossible to do so, then the question will stay closed and will not be answered.

## Search, and research
Before posting a question, we strongly recommend that you spend a reasonable amount of time researching the problem and searching for existing questions on this forums that may provide an answer.


## Write a title that summarises the specific problem
The title is the first thing that potential answerers will see. If your title isn't interesting, they won't read the rest. Also, without a good title, people may not even be able to find your question. So, make the title count:

Pretend you're talking to a busy colleague and have to sum up your entire question in one sentence: what details can you include that will help someone identify and solve your problem? Include any error messages, key APIs, or unusual circumstances that make your question different from similar questions already on the site.

Examples:
> Bad: C# Math Confusion
> Good: Why does using float instead of int give me different results when all of my inputs are integers?
> Bad: [php] session doubt
> Good: How can I redirect users to different pages based on session data in PHP?
> Bad: android if else problems
> Good: Why does str == "value" evaluate to false when str is set to "value"?

## Introduce the problem before you post any code
In the body of your question, start by expanding on the summary you put in the title. Don't just skip straight to the code! It is often helpful to provide some background contextual information, and describing your problem in words is almost as important as describing it with code.

Explain how you encountered the problem you're trying to solve, and any difficulties that have prevented you from solving it yourself. The first paragraph in your question is the second thing most readers will see, so make it as engaging and informative as possible. You want to make a good first impression.

## Help others reproduce the problem
Not all questions benefit from including code, but if your problem is with code you've written, you should include some. But don't just copy in your entire program! Not only is this likely to get you in trouble if you're posting your employer's code, it likely includes a lot of irrelevant details that readers will need to ignore when trying to reproduce the problem. 

### Proofread before posting!
Now that you've finished writing your question, take a deep breath and read through it from start to finish. Pretend you're seeing it for the first time: does it make sense? Can someone without all of your context and background knowledge understand it? Try reproducing the problem yourself, in a fresh environment and make sure you can do so using only the information included in your question. Add any details you missed and read through it again. Re-read your title, and make sure that it succinctly and accurately describes the problem.


## Respond to feedback after posting
After you post, don't run away too fast—leave the question open in your browser for a bit to see if anyone comments. If you missed an obvious piece of information, be ready to respond by editing your question to include it. If someone posts an answer, be ready to try it out and provide feedback!

> In spite of all your efforts, you may find your questions poorly-received. Don't despair! Learning to ask a good question is a worthy pursuit, and not one you'll master overnight.

channels.name: 🐞-zep-helpdesk
channels.topic:
Hi! Please review the following guidelines in order to assist the Zep team and community in answering your support questions.

**Wanting to report a bug?**
Please create a ticket in our GitHub repo: https://github.com/getzep/zep/issues.

**Something Broken?**
Please provide a clear and concise description of what is broken and the steps that led to the broken-ess. Provide code samples, shell commands, log files as necessary. 

Always share what version of Zep you're using, and the versions of client SDKs.
 - Zep version: [e.g. vX.X.X]
 - Zep SDK and version: [e.g. `zep-js` or `zep-python` and vX.X.X]
 - Deployment [e.g. using `docker compose`, to a hosted environment such as Render]

_Note_: The Zep server version is available in the Zep server logs at startup:
`Starting zep server version 0.11.0-cbf4fe4 (2023-08-30T12:49:03+0000)`

**Asking a question?**
Go ahead! But please review the documentation first! https://docs.getzep.com/
